incidents.victim: U.S. critical infrastructure (water, energy sectors)
incidents.vector: Exploited internet-facing PLCs (Rockwell Allen-Bradley) via CVE-2021-22681 to manipulate SCADA/HMI systems at water and energy facilities.
incidents.impact: Operational disruption at multiple U.S. water and energy facilities confirmed by FBI and DoD. Minot, ND water treatment plant directly impacted. Campaign escalated in retaliation for U.S.-Israel military operations against Iran.

incidents.victim: 200+ organizations globally (government agencies, email providers)
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in end-of-life SOHO routers (Mikrotik, TP-Link) to mass-alter DNS settings, enabling adversary-in-the-middle interception of Microsoft Office OAuth tokens post-MFA.
incidents.impact: Over 18,000 routers compromised at peak. Microsoft Office authentication tokens intercepted from foreign affairs ministries and law enforcement agencies across multiple countries.

incidents.victim: DDoS botnet infrastructure (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad)
incidents.vector: Compromised 3M+ IoT devices (routers, webcams) to build DDoS-for-hire botnets targeting hundreds of thousands of victims including U.S. DoD.
incidents.impact: U.S., Canada, and Germany dismantled four botnets and seized infrastructure. Domains and virtual servers seized under court-authorized warrants.

incidents.victim: Freedom Mobile, Rogers, and Fido
incidents.vector: Breach originated at third-party provider Telus Digital and cascaded to multiple Canadian telecom brands.
incidents.impact: Customer data exposed at three major Canadian telecom providers. Customers notified of potential data compromise.

incidents.victim: REvil / GandCrab ransomware victims (130+ organizations globally)
incidents.vector: GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations using double extortion (2018–2021).
incidents.impact: German BKA publicly identified leader 'UNKN' as Russian national Daniil Shchukin. $2 billion extorted globally. U.S. DOJ previously seized $317K in cryptocurrency. Co-conspirator also named.

incidents.victim: Fortinet customers globally
incidents.vector: Unauthenticated remote code execution zero-day (CVE-2026-35616, CVSS 9.8) in FortiClient EMS, actively exploited since March 31 with significant escalation by April 6.
incidents.impact: Nearly 2,000 exposed instances identified. Emergency hotfix issued over Easter weekend; full patch still pending. Added to CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.

incidents.victim: Jones Day (major U.S. law firm)
incidents.vector: Successful phishing attack leading to unauthorized access to firm systems.
incidents.impact: Client materials posted online by attackers. Investigation ongoing at one of the largest U.S. law firms (2,400+ lawyers).

incidents.victim: FBI Director Kash Patel (personal data claimed)
incidents.vector: Claimed compromise of personal data (method unconfirmed).
incidents.impact: FBI stated no government information was taken. Claim credibility disputed — group known for exaggerating impact.

incidents.victim: Education Authority of Northern Ireland (C2K network)
incidents.vector: Cyberattack on C2K centralized school IT network (specific vector not disclosed).
incidents.impact: Approximately 300,000 pupils and 20,000 teachers lost access to teaching materials, assignments, and communication tools during exam period. Recovery ongoing.

incidents.victim: St. Joseph County, Indiana
incidents.vector: Compromise of county external fax service.
incidents.impact: Limited operational impact. Handala group claimed credit as part of broader anti-U.S. campaign.

incidents.victim: Statistics South Africa
incidents.vector: Ransomware deployment by XP95 group (new group, active since March 2026).
incidents.impact: Government statistics agency extorted for $100,000 not to leak stolen data.

incidents.victim: Multiple organizations (VMware vSphere environments)
incidents.vector: Exploited weak security architecture and identity design flaws in VMware vCenter Server Appliance to install BRICKSTORM malware at the Tier-0 virtualization layer, below EDR visibility.
incidents.impact: Attackers gained administrative control over all managed ESXi hosts and VMs. Mandiant published defender's guide and hardening script in response to ongoing campaign.

incidents.victim: Israeli and UAE municipalities, satellite, aviation, energy, and maritime organizations
incidents.vector: Wide-ranging Microsoft 365 password spray campaign targeting organizations in cities also hit by Iranian missile strikes, assessed as bombing damage assessment intelligence gathering.
incidents.impact: Three waves of attacks since early March 2026 targeting Israel, UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Europe. Campaign directly supports Iranian kinetic military operations.

incidents.victim: Axios npm package users (100M+ weekly downloads)
incidents.vector: Social engineering of package maintainer via fake trading company and video call, leading to npm account takeover and injection of WAVESHAPER.V2 backdoor into versions 1.14.1 and 0.30.4.
incidents.impact: Trojanized versions published with remote access backdoor targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux. Malicious versions live for approximately 3 hours before detection and removal.

incidents.victim: Government officials and high-risk individuals in UK and allied countries
incidents.vector: Social engineering to obtain login and recovery codes for WhatsApp, Signal, and Messenger accounts; adding attacker devices to victim accounts; phishing via malicious QR codes.
incidents.impact: NCSC and international partners issued joint advisory warning of growing campaign targeting individuals with access to sensitive government information.

incidents.victim: Organizations using F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager
incidents.vector: Unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability (CVE-2025-53521) in F5 BIG-IP APM, actively exploited in the wild.
incidents.impact: NCSC issued urgent advisory to isolate, investigate for compromise, and rebuild affected systems. F5 published indicators of compromise.

incidents.victim: Uranium Finance (decentralized crypto exchange)
incidents.vector: Smart contract exploitation across 26 liquidity pools in April 2021, stealing $53.3 million and forcing permanent platform shutdown.
incidents.impact: Indictment of Jonathan Spalletta unsealed March 30, 2026. Approximately $31 million seized by law enforcement in February 2025. Funds were laundered via Tornado Cash.

incidents.victim: Israeli and Gulf state critical infrastructure operators
incidents.vector: GRU published stolen access credentials to Israeli critical infrastructure control systems; Iranian hacking groups leveraged Russian VPS infrastructure and attack techniques for follow-on operations.
incidents.impact: Iranian groups used Russian-provided techniques and infrastructure to target energy and telecom companies in Israel and Gulf states. Russian satellite intelligence also used to guide Iranian kinetic strikes.

incidents.victim: Southeast Asian government and military targets
incidents.vector: Multiple converging threat clusters deployed advanced backdoors including AppleChris against government and military entities in Southeast Asia.
incidents.impact: Coordinated or overlapping espionage operations by multiple China-nexus actors targeting the same organizations, suggesting state-directed intelligence collection.

incidents.victim: Drift (cryptocurrency trading platform)
incidents.vector: Long-term social engineering via fake trading company and conference meetings, followed by device compromise using malicious Apple TestFlight app and VS Code extensions to access multi-signature wallet controls.
incidents.impact: $280 million stolen from platform treasury. Attack attributed to the same North Korean unit responsible for previous cryptocurrency exchange thefts.

incidents.victim: Users of Trivy, LiteLLM, and other open-source packages
incidents.vector: Compromised Aqua Security Trivy GitHub Actions releases on March 19, then cascaded through stolen CI/CD credentials to LiteLLM, Checkmarx, and other packages via GitHub Actions, PyPI, and npm.
incidents.impact: Estimated 300 GB of credentials stolen with >10,000:1 fan-out ratio. CanisterWorm wiper deployed against systems with Iranian locale. Campaign shifted to monetization via Vect ransomware. CISA added CVE-2026-33634 to KEV catalog.

incidents.victim: Stryker Corporation
incidents.vector: Wiper malware deployed against corporate systems in retaliation for U.S.-Israel military operations against Iran.
incidents.impact: 200,000 company devices wiped. Order processing, manufacturing, and shipping disrupted for approximately three weeks. Company reported full recovery by early April 2026.
